2605.04857.title: Assessing Cognitive Effort in L2 Idiomatic Processing: An Eye-Tracking Dataset
2605.04857.paper_summary: This paper presents the development and validation of an eye-tracking dataset designed to investigate how second-language (L2) learners process idiomatic expressions. While native speakers often rely on direct retrieval of figurative meanings, L2 speakers frequently adopt a literal-first approach, which incurs measurable cognitive costs. This resource captures these costs through ocular metrics recorded from Portuguese L1 speakers of English across all CEFR proficiency levels (A1-C2). Although the study uses entry-level 60 Hz hardware (Tobii Pro Spark), we demonstrate that this sampling rate provides sufficient data density to detect macro-cognitive events such as fixations and regressions in reading. Preliminary analysis validates the dataset by revealing a strong inverse correlation between language proficiency and regressive eye movements. Integrated into the MIA (Modeling Idiomaticity in Human and Artificial Language Processing) initiative, this dataset serves as a cognitively grounded benchmark for evaluating both human processing models and the alignment of large language models with human-like figurative understanding.
2605.04857.paper_summary_zh: 這篇論文介紹了一個眼動追蹤數據集的開發與驗證，旨在研究第二語言 (L2) 學習者如何處理成語表達。雖然母語使用者通常依賴直接檢索比喻意義，但L2使用者經常採用字面優先的方法，這會產生可測量的認知成本。這個資源通過從所有CEFR熟練度級別 (A1-C2) 的葡萄牙語母語者中記錄的眼動指標來捕捉這些成本。儘管該研究使用的是入門級的60 Hz硬體 (Tobii Pro Spark)，我們證明這個取樣率提供了足夠的數據密度來檢測閱讀中的宏觀認知事件，如注視和回退。初步分析通過揭示語言熟練度與回退眼動之間的強負相關性來驗證數據集。這個數據集整合進MIA (人類與人工語言處理中的成語建模) 計劃，作為一個基於認知的基準，用於評估人類處理模型和大型語言模型與人類類似比喻理解的對齊。
2605.04857.author: Eduardo Santos et.al.
2605.04857.authors: Eduardo Santos, Juliana Carvalho, César Rennó-Costa
